summary: Creates an ongoing conversation between King’s and every student about their purpose, ambitions, needs and direction, helping them take ownership of their journey and navigate opportunities to learn, connect and contribute.
detailSummary:
From accepting an offer through to graduation, students regularly reflect on what matters to them, what they hope to achieve and contribute, where they need connection or support, and how their direction is changing. Purpose spans learning, personal development, belonging, service, experience and future life, with uncertainty recognised as a legitimate starting point.

At its centre is the King’s Canvas, a living, self-curated account of the experience each student wants to build. It connects academic learning with wider opportunities, relationships, communities and ambitions. Digital tools and carefully bounded uses of AI sustain this at scale, while existing human relationships provide judgement, challenge and care at meaningful moments.

Insight into students’ ambitions and needs enables King’s to recommend relevant opportunities and make better-informed decisions about enrichment provision.
problemSolved: Universities promise wider personal development but tend to understand student journeys through courses and institutional processes, leaving individual purpose implicit and underassured. At King’s, a rich but fragmented opportunity ecology is difficult to navigate and rewards students who already possess confidence, networks and institutional knowledge.
whatChanges: Every student is supported to develop and revise a sense of purpose, connect their course with the wider King’s experience and make intentional choices. King’s uses insight into students’ ambitions and needs to connect them with relevant opportunities and shape provision more responsively.

caseForChange.problem:
Universities promise more than disciplinary knowledge. They are places where students develop direction, relationships and broad capabilities as thinkers, collaborators, creators and citizens. Yet much of this value remains implicit and underassured, treated as something students absorb rather than something every student is supported to shape.

Student journeys are also understood primarily through courses and institutional processes. The course provides an essential academic spine, but it cannot fully represent an individual trajectory that may cross disciplines or lead towards a future only loosely connected to the subject studied.

King’s has a rich ecology of academic, co-curricular and extra-curricular opportunities, but students can find it fragmented and difficult to navigate. Those with greater confidence, networks or institutional knowledge are better placed to identify what matters and how experiences connect, reinforcing inequality and fear of missing out.
caseForChange.opportunity:
King’s can make individual purpose a visible and supported part of university life, helping students connect their course with wider ambitions for learning, belonging, development, service and future direction.

Purpose includes how students relate to the world beyond themselves, the communities to which they belong and the contribution their knowledge and capabilities might enable them to make.

The King’s Canvas gives this individual trajectory a living form. Technology enables regular reflection and opportunity discovery at scale, existing relationships provide human support, and patterns in student ambitions help King’s shape provision more responsively.
caseForChange.whyNow:
Students increasingly bear the cost of higher education, with international students making a particularly significant investment. Economic, technological and cultural change is disrupting established graduate pathways and intensifying scrutiny of the graduate premium.

King’s needs to make the wider value of university intentional, visible and equitable. This gives contemporary expression to knowledge with purpose and its tradition of service, helping students consider what they want from their education and what they might contribute through it.
caseForChange.intendedChange:
Every student is supported to develop and revise a sense of purpose, connect their course with the wider King’s experience and make intentional choices about their journey.

Technology enables this at scale, while existing relationships provide support at meaningful points. King’s connects students with relevant opportunities and uses their ambitions and needs to shape future provision.
